title: Northstar Capital Style Rules
summary: House writing standards for all client-facing and IC-facing output.
canonical_context: Write in concise, high-signal bullets.
Avoid hype language and promotional adjectives.
Separate thesis, evidence, risks, and next steps.
State confidence level when evidence is incomplete.
Mark source limitations explicitly.

title: Investment Research Department
summary: Norms for diligence, IC discussion, and client-ready memos.
canonical_context: Outputs should support diligence, IC discussion, and client-ready memos.
Claims should be tied to source facts when possible.
Risks should be surfaced before recommendations.

title: Healthcare AI Diligence — Objective
summary: Evaluate Clinia Health, AI workflow software for provider organizations.
canonical_context: The project evaluates Clinia Health: an AI drafting layer on Epic/Cerner for prior-auth packets, referrals, and post-visit summaries.
Key concerns: ROI, implementation burden, privacy, hallucination risk, EHR integration, and defensibility.
The single largest risk is the Epic partnership dependency (termination rights, native feature overlap).
Audience for outputs: investment committee and, later, LPs.

must_not: State the payer licensing rumor as fact

title: Diligence Memo Workflow
summary: Turn research notes into IC-ready memo bullets.
canonical_context: Repeatable process: raw research notes → client-ready memo bullets.
IC reads memos in under 5 minutes; keep every section to at most 4 bullets.
Unverified numbers must be flagged inline, not footnoted.

must: Keep sections to 4 bullets or fewer

title: Client-Ready Bullet Memo
summary: Expected output artifact: sectioned markdown bullet memo.
canonical_context: Output is markdown with H2 sections and short bullets.
Required sections come from the applied skill's output contract.
Tone: neutral, analytical, no adjectives that sell.

title: Source Limitation Guardrail
summary: Deterministic constraints on sourcing and claims.
canonical_context: Every quantitative claim must trace to the dataroom, a reference call, or be flagged as unverified.
Company-provided metrics (NPS, hallucination rate, the 78% labor-reduction slide) must be labeled self-reported.
Confidential details (customer names, the payer licensing approach) must not appear in client-facing output.

must_not: Name reference customers in client-facing output

title: Clinia Health — Research Notes (compiled facts)
summary: Compact fact card distilled from the raw notes dump (calls, refs, dataroom).
canonical_context: Clinia Health: AI drafting layer on Epic/Cerner for prior-auth, referrals, post-visit summaries; raising $40M Series B at $320M pre.
ARR: $17.2M per dataroom vs $18.4M CEO claim — unreconciled; NDR 118%, gross logo retention 94%, burn $1.4M/mo, 21 months runway.

Reference calls: 40-clinic ortho group strong (prior-auth 5.2 → 1.9 days, 3-yr renewal at +20% price) but 5-month implementation vs 6 weeks promised; regional CMIO stalled at 150-seat pilot pending an Epic version upgrade.
Hallucination rate 2.3% of drafts (internal eval, all drafts clinician-reviewed before sending; methodology unvalidated).
Core risk: Epic partnership dependency — termination rights unknown; Epic building overlapping native features.
Margin: 71% claimed gross margin recomputes to 64-66% with inference and deployment engineering allocated.

title: Enterprise Software Diligence — Objective
summary: Evaluate Relay CI, developer productivity analytics for mid-size engineering orgs.
canonical_context: The project evaluates Relay CI: developer productivity analytics (DORA metrics, review latency, incident cost) for 200-2,000-engineer orgs.
Key concerns: expansion economics, data-access friction with security teams, and GitHub/GitLab platform risk.
Deal stage: post-term-sheet, confirmatory diligence for the upcoming board discussion.

must: Tie usage claims to product telemetry shared in the dataroom

title: Relay CI — Deal Facts
summary: Compact fact card for the Relay CI confirmatory diligence.
canonical_context: Relay CI: $9.8M ARR growing 96% YoY; 132% NDR; 61 customers, median 480 engineers per customer.
Term sheet signed: $25M Series A extension at $145M pre; confirmatory diligence closes in 3 weeks.
Wins this quarter: two 1,500+ engineer logos closed; SOC2 Type II issued; GitHub Marketplace listing approved.
Open risks: single-threaded champion in 70% of accounts; GitHub API rate-limit changes could raise COGS ~4 points; VP Sales seat open for 5 months.
Asks in flight: intro to a design-partner CISO; board seat observer confirmation.
